title: Compound Interest Calculator — Daily, Monthly, Yearly & With Deposits
description: Calculate compound interest with daily, monthly, quarterly, or annual compounding. Add deposits, withdrawals, and see your future value over time.
headline: Compound interest calculator with deposits and growth table
subhead: Free, no signup — calculate future value, total contributions, and interest earned directly in your browser.

presets.label: Default example: $10,000 principal, 7% annual return, $100 monthly contribution

intro: Compound interest grows your money by earning interest on both your original principal and the interest already earned. Over time, even small regular deposits can make a large difference.

Use this calculator to estimate future value from an initial amount, recurring contributions, annual interest rate, and compounding frequency. The result includes your total principal, total interest, and a year-by-year growth table.

tutorial.title: How to calculate compound interest
steps: Enter your starting principal or initial investment amount.
Add an optional monthly or yearly contribution if you plan to deposit more over time.
Set the annual interest rate, number of years, and compounding frequency.
Click calculate to see future value, total contributions, total interest, and yearly growth.
Adjust the inputs to compare daily, monthly, quarterly, or annual compounding scenarios.

faq.question: What formula does this compound interest calculator use?
faq.answer: For the starting principal, it uses A = P(1 + r/n)^(nt). Regular deposits are added at the end of each contribution period and compounded forward to the end of the term.

faq.question: Can I calculate compound interest with monthly contributions?
faq.answer: Yes. Enter a contribution amount and choose monthly contributions. The calculator adds each deposit over the term and shows the combined future value.

faq.question: What is the difference between daily, monthly, and annual compounding?
faq.answer: More frequent compounding applies interest more often. Daily compounding usually produces a slightly higher future value than monthly or annual compounding at the same annual rate.

faq.question: Is this result financial advice?
faq.answer: No. This is an educational estimate. Actual returns, bank rules, taxes, fees, inflation, and market performance can change the final result.

disclaimer: This calculator is for educational estimates only and does not account for taxes, fees, or investment risk.
keywords: compound interest calculator with monthly contributions
